records.ety: [AS. behabban to surround, restrain, detain (akin to G. gehaben (obs.) to have, sich gehaben to behave or carry one's self); pref. be- + habban to have. See Have, v. t. ]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Behaved (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Behaving.]

defs: To manage or govern in point of behavior; to discipline; to handle; to restrain.
quotes: He did behave his anger ere 't was spent.

defs: To carry; to conduct; to comport; to manage; to bear; -- used reflexively.
To act; to conduct; to bear or carry one's self; as, to behave well or ill.
notes: ☞ This verb is often used colloquially without an adverb of manner; as, if he does not behave, he will be punished. It is also often applied to inanimate objects; as, the ship behaved splendidly.
